title: Planck-Einstein energy-frequency relation
description: A photon of frequency f has energy E = h f; more generally quantum stationary-state phases evolve with angular frequency E/hbar.
assumptions: For E = h f, the excitation is a photon or a mode quantum
Frequency is defined in the relevant inertial frame

evidence.source: Photoelectric effect, spectroscopy, Compton scattering, Josephson and quantum electrical standards

primary_sources: Einstein, Ueber einen die Erzeugung und Verwandlung des Lichtes betreffenden heuristischen Gesichtspunkt (1905)
falsification_conditions: Precision photon-energy measurements show a reproducible nonlinearity not explained by known physics
confusion_guard: E = h f does not by itself assign invariant rest mass m = h f / c^2 to a photon; a photon has zero invariant mass.
